title: One line of math froze my AI agent forever. The timeout watched and did nothing.
excerpt: This is my second entry for the DEV x Sentry Bug Smash challenge. Entry #1 was a crash with a...
content_html:
This is a submission for DEV's Summer Bug Smash: Clear the Lineup powered by Sentry.

This is my second entry for the DEV x Sentry Bug Smash challenge. Entry #1 was a crash with a confusing error message. This one is the opposite and it is scarier: no crash, no message, no event. Just silence.

The bug that sends you nothing

smolagents runs LLM-generated Python in a sandboxed executor with a timeout. Issue #2473 claims that one line of model-generated math defeats it completely:

from smolagents.local_python_executor import LocalPythonExecutor

executor = LocalPythonExecutor(additional_authorized_imports=[], timeout_seconds=2)
executor.send_tools({})
executor("10 ** 10 ** 8")  # 2 second timeout. Should be fine, right?

I ran this with a 2 second timeout and a faulthandler bomb set for 20 seconds. The timeout never fired. The process sat frozen until the external kill. An agent that generates this expression (and "compute this huge number" is exactly the kind of thing agents try) freezes its host process forever.

Zero comments on the issue, zero PRs. Mine now.

Why the timeout lies to you

smolagents' timeout is thread based: a worker thread runs the code, the main thread waits in future.result(timeout=2). That design is fine for almost everything, because CPython switches threads between bytecode instructions.

But 10 ** 10 ** 8 is not "almost everything". CPython computes arbitrary precision **, << and * inside a single C call that holds the GIL from start to finish. No bytecode boundary, no thread switch, no timeout. The result would have about 400 million bits. The computation takes somewhere between minutes and hours. Your watchdog needs the GIL to wake up, and it never gets it.

The faulthandler dump made it concrete, and it was worse than the issue described:

Thread 0x16d1f3000 (worker):
  File "local_python_executor.py", line 753 in evaluate_binop   <- computing the pow

Thread 0x1f00d5e80 (main):
  File "threading.py", line 999 in start
  File "concurrent/futures/thread.py", line 180 in submit       <- never returned!

The main thread was still stuck inside ThreadPoolExecutor.submit. It never even reached future.result. The 2 second timer never armed at all.

The existing MAX_OPERATIONS guard (10 million AST operations) does not help either. This is a handful of AST nodes. The entire cost lives inside one of them.

The Sentry angle: monitoring for absence

Entry #1 was about a noisy failure. This bug is the opposite. I pointed a fresh Sentry project at a simulated agent run on the unpatched PyPI release (1.26.0) and got the most unsettling result possible: nothing. No event, no transaction, an empty project. The process was frozen mid-transaction and the SDK never got a chance to flush.

The lesson: for freeze-class bugs you need a supervisor. I added a small watchdog process that gives the worker 25 seconds, then kills it and reports what it saw, with the worker's faulthandler stack attached as evidence:

watchdog: starting worker (before) with 25s budget
worker: smolagents 1.26.0
worker: step 1 executing 'result = 10 ** 10 ** 8'...
watchdog: worker FROZE, killed it, reporting to Sentry

The resulting Sentry issue carries the whole story in one place: environment tagged before, handled by the supervisor, with the frozen frame (evaluate_binop, line 753 of local_python_executor.py) sitting in the attached worker_faulthandler_stack extra.

Sentry's Seer ran root cause analysis on that issue and independently landed on the same conclusion: signal and thread interruption need the GIL, and a single C-level big-int operation never releases it.

Seer's verdict, verbatim:

CodeAgent's 2s timeout uses Python signal-based interruption, which cannot fire during uninterruptible C-level big-int operations that hold the GIL. [...] A single large big-integer arithmetic operation runs entirely as one C-level call that holds the GIL continuously without yielding. CPython cannot deliver signals or switch threads during an uninterruptible C extension call, so no timeout callback fires for the duration of that operation.

The fix: you cannot interrupt it, so refuse to start it

Killing the computation mid-flight is impossible from Python. But predicting the damage is O(1). Before executing **, << or * on integers, the executor now estimates the result's bit length from the operands' bit lengths:

if op == "**":
    estimated_bits = left.bit_length() * right       # upper bound
elif op == "<<":
    estimated_bits = left.bit_length() + right
elif op == "*":
    estimated_bits = left.bit_length() + right.bit_length()

Above 1 million bits (about 300k digits, still generous) it raises an informative InterpreterError:

Operation '**' would produce an integer of around 400000000 bits, exceeding
the maximum of 1000000 bits allowed. Use smaller operands, or
pow(base, exp, mod) for modular exponentiation.

That message matters. The agent surfaces it to the model, and the model can actually act on it: use pow(base, exp, mod), which stays unrestricted because modular exponentiation is fast and legitimate. The agent recovers on the next step instead of hanging the host.

The after run on the patched build: the guard rejects the expression in 0.0 seconds, the error lands in Sentry as a normal actionable issue and step 2 executes fine.

watchdog: starting worker (after) with 25s budget
worker: smolagents 1.27.0.dev0
worker: step 1 error surfaced to the model: InterpreterError: ...
worker: step 2 executing '2 + 2'...
worker: step 2 ok
worker: DONE
watchdog: worker exited with code 0

A robot reviewed my robot fix

Minutes after I opened the PR, OpenAI's Codex reviewer flagged a real hole: my guard checked type(x) is int, which lets bool and int subclasses slip through. True << 10**9 and class BigInt(int) still reached the uninterruptible C calls. Fixed with isinstance, added both as regression tests. AI found the bug class, AI fixed it, AI reviewed the fix. I just steered.

Numbers

  • Freeze reproduced at 20+ seconds (would have run for hours), external kill required
  • Fix rejects the same expression in 0.0 seconds
  • 9 explosive patterns blocked: **, <<, chained *, all augmented forms, pow(a, b), bool and int subclass variants
  • 10 legitimate operations verified untouched: 100! via repeated *=, pow(7, 2**64, 97), float pow, 1 ** 10**9
  • 19 new tests, full test file 416 passed, ruff clean
  • The blocked-pattern tests hang forever on unpatched main. I verified that the honest way, with a stash and a kill switch.

Links

  • Issue: https://github.com/huggingface/smolagents/issues/2473
  • PR: https://github.com/huggingface/smolagents/pull/2551
  • Entry #1: https://dev.to/himanshu_748/i-fixed-a-smolagents-bug-that-confused-everyone-who-hit-it-with-sentry-watching-the-whole-time-1im

The scariest bugs are not the ones that page you at 3am. They are the ones that make sure nothing ever pages you at all. Instrument for silence.

blocks.html_en: smolagents' timeout is thread based: a worker thread runs the code, the main thread waits in future.result(timeout=2). That design is fine for almost everything, because CPython switches threads between bytecode instructions.

sentences.zh: smolagents 的超时是基于线程的：工作线程运行代码，主线程在 future.result(timeout=2) 中等待。

sentences.en: That design is fine for almost everything, because CPython switches threads between bytecode instructions.
sentences.zh: 这种设计几乎适用于所有情况，因为 CPython 在字节码指令之间切换线程。

blocks.html_en: But 10 ** 10 ** 8 is not "almost everything". CPython computes arbitrary precision **, << and * inside a single C call that holds the GIL from start to finish. No bytecode boundary, no thread switch, no timeout. The result would have about 400 million bits. The computation takes somewhere between minutes and hours. Your watchdog needs the GIL to wake up, and it never gets it.

sentences.zh: 但 10 ** 10 ** 8 并不是“几乎一切”。

sentences.en: CPython computes arbitrary precision **, << and * inside a single C call that holds the GIL from start to finish.
sentences.zh: CPython 在从头到尾保存 GIL 的单个 C 调用中计算任意精度 **、<< 和 *。

sentences.en: No bytecode boundary, no thread switch, no timeout.
sentences.zh: 无字节码边界、无线程切换、无超时。

sentences.en: The result would have about 400 million bits.
sentences.zh: 结果大约有 4 亿位。

sentences.en: The computation takes somewhere between minutes and hours.
sentences.zh: 计算需要几分钟到几小时之间的时间。

sentences.en: Your watchdog needs the GIL to wake up, and it never gets it.
sentences.zh: 你的看门狗需要 GIL 来唤醒，但它永远无法唤醒。

sentences.zh: 教训：对于冻结级错误，您需要一名主管。

sentences.zh: 我添加了一个小的看门狗进程，该进程给了工作线程 25 秒的时间，然后杀死它并报告它所看到的内容，并附上工作线程的故障处理程序堆栈作为证据：

sentences.zh: Sentry 的 Seer 对这个问题进行了根本原因分析，并独立得出了相同的结论：信号和线程中断需要 GIL，而单个 C 级 big-int 操作永远不会释放它。

sentences.zh: 单个大型大整数算术运算完全作为一个 C 级调用运行，该调用连续持有 GIL 而不会产生任何结果。

sentences.en: CPython cannot deliver signals or switch threads during an uninterruptible C extension call, so no timeout callback fires for the duration of that operation.
sentences.zh: CPython 无法在不间断的 C 扩展调用期间传递信号或切换线程，因此在该操作期间不会触发超时回调。

blocks.html_en: Killing the computation mid-flight is impossible from Python. But predicting the damage is O(1). Before executing **, << or * on integers, the executor now estimates the result's bit length from the operands' bit lengths:

sentences.en: Killing the computation mid-flight is impossible from Python.
sentences.zh: 对于 Python 来说，中止计算是不可能的。

sentences.en: But predicting the damage is O(1).
sentences.zh: 但预测损坏的时间复杂度为 O(1)。

sentences.en: Before executing **, << or * on integers, the executor now estimates the result's bit length from the operands' bit lengths:

blocks.html_en: That message matters. The agent surfaces it to the model, and the model can actually act on it: use pow(base, exp, mod), which stays unrestricted because modular exponentiation is fast and legitimate. The agent recovers on the next step instead of hanging the host.

sentences.en: That message matters.
sentences.zh: 这个消息很重要。

sentences.en: The agent surfaces it to the model, and the model can actually act on it: use pow(base, exp, mod), which stays unrestricted because modular exponentiation is fast and legitimate.
sentences.zh: 代理将其呈现给模型，模型实际上可以对其进行操作：使用 pow(base, exp, mod)，它不受限制，因为模幂运算快速且合法。

sentences.en: The agent recovers on the next step instead of hanging the host.

blocks.html_en: The after run on the patched build: the guard rejects the expression in 0.0 seconds, the error lands in Sentry as a normal actionable issue and step 2 executes fine.

blocks.html_en: A robot reviewed my robot fix

blocks.html_en: Minutes after I opened the PR, OpenAI's Codex reviewer flagged a real hole: my guard checked type(x) is int, which lets bool and int subclasses slip through. True << 10**9 and class BigInt(int) still reached the uninterruptible C calls. Fixed with isinstance, added both as regression tests. AI found the bug class, AI fixed it, AI reviewed the fix. I just steered.

sentences.en: Minutes after I opened the PR, OpenAI's Codex reviewer flagged a real hole: my guard checked type(x) is int, which lets bool and int subclasses slip through.
sentences.zh: 在我打开 PR 几分钟后，OpenAI 的 Codex 审阅者标记了一个真正的漏洞：我的守卫检查的 type(x) 是 int，这让 bool 和 int 子类溜了过去。

blocks.html_en: The scariest bugs are not the ones that page you at 3am. They are the ones that make sure nothing ever pages you at all. Instrument for silence.

sentences.en: The scariest bugs are not the ones that page you at 3am.
sentences.zh: 最可怕的虫子并不是凌晨 3 点向你寻呼的虫子。

sentences.en: They are the ones that make sure nothing ever pages you at all.
sentences.zh: 他们可以确保没有任何事情骚扰你。

sentences.en: Instrument for silence.
sentences.zh: 沉默的乐器。

content_html_zh:
这是为 DEV 的 Summer Bug Smash: Clear the Lineup 提交的内容，由 Sentry 提供支持。

这是我参加 DEV x Sentry Bug Smash 挑战赛的第二篇作品。 条目 #1 是一次崩溃，并带有令人困惑的错误消息。 这个则相反，而且更可怕：没有崩溃，没有消息，没有事件。 只是沉默。

该错误不会向您发送任何信息

smolagents 在沙盒执行器中运行 LLM 生成的 Python，并设置超时。 Issue #2473 声称模型生成的数学公式完全击败了它：

from smolagents.local_python_executor import LocalPythonExecutor

executor = LocalPythonExecutor(additional_authorized_imports=[], timeout_seconds=2)
executor.send_tools({})
executor("10 ** 10 ** 8")  # 2 second timeout. Should be fine, right?

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

我运行此程序时设置了 2 秒超时，并设置了 20 秒的故障处理程序炸弹。 超时从未触发。 该进程一直处于冻结状态，直到外部终止为止。 生成此表达式的代理（并且“计算这个巨大的数字”正是代理尝试的那种事情）永远冻结其主机进程。

对这个问题的零评论，零 PR。 现在是我的了。

为什么超时对你撒谎

smolagents 的超时是基于线程的：工作线程运行代码，主线程在 future.result(timeout=2) 中等待。 这种设计几乎适用于所有情况，因为 CPython 在字节码指令之间切换线程。

但 10 ** 10 ** 8 并不是“几乎一切”。 CPython 在从头到尾保存 GIL 的单个 C 调用中计算任意精度 **、<< 和 *。 无字节码边界、无线程切换、无超时。 结果大约有 4 亿位。 计算需要几分钟到几小时之间的时间。 你的看门狗需要 GIL 来唤醒，但它永远无法唤醒。

故障处理程序转储使其变得具体，并且比所描述的问题更糟糕：

Thread 0x16d1f3000 (worker):
  File "local_python_executor.py", line 753 in evaluate_binop   <- computing the pow

Thread 0x1f00d5e80 (main):
  File "threading.py", line 999 in start
  File "concurrent/futures/thread.py", line 180 in submit       <- never returned!

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

主线程仍然卡在 ThreadPoolExecutor.submit 中。 它甚至从未达到 future.result。 2 秒计时器根本没有启动。

现有的 MAX_OPERATIONS 保护（1000 万 AST 操作）也没有帮助。 这是一些 AST 节点。 全部成本都包含在其中一个中。

哨兵角度：监控缺席情况

条目 #1 是关于一次吵闹的失败。 这个错误则相反。 我将一个新的 Sentry 项目指向在未修补的 PyPI 版本 (1.26.0) 上运行的模拟代理，并得到了最令人不安的结果：没有任何结果。 没有事件，没有交易，一个空项目。 该进程在事务中被冻结，SDK 从未有机会刷新。

教训：对于冻结级错误，您需要一名主管。 我添加了一个小的看门狗进程，该进程给了工作线程 25 秒的时间，然后杀死它并报告它所看到的内容，并附上工作线程的故障处理程序堆栈作为证据：

watchdog: starting worker (before) with 25s budget
worker: smolagents 1.26.0
worker: step 1 executing 'result = 10 ** 10 ** 8'...
watchdog: worker FROZE, killed it, reporting to Sentry

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

由此产生的 Sentry 问题将整个故事集中在一个地方：之前标记的环境，由主管处理，冻结帧（evaluate_binop，local_python_executor.py 的第 753 行）位于附加的worker_faulthandler_stack extra中。

Sentry 的 Seer 对这个问题进行了根本原因分析，并独立得出了相同的结论：信号和线程中断需要 GIL，而单个 C 级 big-int 操作永远不会释放它。

先知的判决一字不差：

CodeAgent 的 2 秒超时使用 Python 基于信号的中断，在持有 GIL 的不间断 C 级 big-int 操作期间无法触发。 [...] 单个大型大整数算术运算完全作为一个 C 级调用运行，该调用连续持有 GIL 而不会产生任何结果。 CPython 无法在不间断的 C 扩展调用期间传递信号或切换线程，因此在该操作期间不会触发超时回调。

修复：您无法中断它，因此拒绝启动它

对于 Python 来说，中止计算是不可能的。 但预测损坏的时间复杂度为 O(1)。 在对整数执行 **、<< 或 * 之前，执行器现在根据操作数的位长度估计结果的位长度：

if op == "**":
    estimated_bits = left.bit_length() * right       # upper bound
elif op == "<<":
    estimated_bits = left.bit_length() + right
elif op == "*":
    estimated_bits = left.bit_length() + right.bit_length()

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

超过 100 万位（大约 30 万位，仍然很慷慨），它会引发一个信息丰富的 InterpreterError：

Operation '**' would produce an integer of around 400000000 bits, exceeding
the maximum of 1000000 bits allowed. Use smaller operands, or
pow(base, exp, mod) for modular exponentiation.

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

这个消息很重要。 代理将其呈现给模型，模型实际上可以对其进行操作：使用 pow(base, exp, mod)，它不受限制，因为模幂运算快速且合法。 代理将在下一步中恢复，而不是挂起主机。

在修补后的版本上运行后：守卫在 0.0 秒内拒绝表达式，错误作为正常的可操作问题出现在 Sentry 中，并且步骤 2 执行正常。

watchdog: starting worker (after) with 25s budget
worker: smolagents 1.27.0.dev0
worker: step 1 error surfaced to the model: InterpreterError: ...
worker: step 2 executing '2 + 2'...
worker: step 2 ok
worker: DONE
watchdog: worker exited with code 0

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

机器人检查了我的机器人修复

在我打开 PR 几分钟后，OpenAI 的 Codex 审阅者标记了一个真正的漏洞：我的守卫检查的 type(x) 是 int，这让 bool 和 int 子类溜了过去。 True << 10**9 和类 BigInt(int) 仍然达到不间断的 C 调用。 使用 isinstance 进行了修复，并将两者添加为回归测试。 AI 发现了错误类别，AI 修复了它，AI 审查了修复。 我刚刚驾驶。

数字

  • 冻结在 20 秒以上重现（将运行数小时），需要外部终止
  • 修复 0.0 秒内拒绝相同表达式的问题
  • 阻止 9 种爆炸性模式：**、<<、链式 *、所有增强形式、pow(a, b)、bool 和 int 子类变体
  • 10 个合法操作已验证未受影响：100！ 通过重复 *=, pow(7, 2**64, 97), float pow, 1 ** 10**9
  • 19个新测试，完整测试文件416个通过，ruff干净
  • 阻塞模式测试永远挂在未打补丁的主干线上。 我用一个藏匿处和一个终止开关以诚实的方式验证了这一点。

Links

  • 问题：https://github.com/huggingface/smolagents/issues/2473
  • 公关：https://github.com/huggingface/smolagents/pull/2551
  • 条目#1：https://dev.to/himanshu_748/i-fixed-a-smolagents-bug-that-c​​onfused-everyone-who-hit-it-with-sentry-watching-the-whole-time-1im

最可怕的虫子并不是凌晨 3 点向你寻呼的虫子。 他们可以确保没有任何事情骚扰你。 沉默的乐器。
